text: Hashing has attracted increasing attention in the field of efficient similarity search in large-scale multimedia databases, due to its storage-saving property and fast search speed. To further explore the underlying relationship between different modalities and achieve better retrieval performance, many supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) algorithms have been proposed. Unfortunately, most existing works spend a lot of time in the training procedure, especially when the training dataset is large, i.e., their time complexity is generally of order O(n 2 ), which is still unacceptable for large-scale data. In this paper, we propose a new efficient framework called Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM), which can incorporate semantic information to facilitate the exploration of underlying relationships between data modalities efficiently, and therefore avoid the costly construction of a large similarity matrix. In the proposed SCM, we present two different hashing learning methods: Orthogonal Projection-based Hashing (OPH) and Sequential Hashing (SH). The OPH model employs orthogonal projection to ensure the orthogonal property in the hash code, while the SH model explores each hash code bit sequentially with the closed-form solution to ease the search of optimal hyperparameters. The experiments on Wiki and NUS-WIDE datasets show that SCM outperforms all the state-of-the-art multimodal hashing algorithms with a remarkable speedup, confirming its scalability.
